danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar reducing to critical threshold
Life gauge flashing red at 25% or below
danger_signals.meaning: Fighter in critical danger state, high-risk/high-reward moment

danger_signals.visual_form: Electrified wires, bull stampede in adjacent plane
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threat if knocked to that plane

danger_signals.visual_form: Facing increasingly powerful non-playable bosses after playable rounds
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level in single-player campaign

safety_signals.visual_form: Full or high health bar display
Fighter at screen distance, back dash mechanic visible
safety_signals.meaning: Spatial safety through plane switching or repositioning

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character-specific fighting styles and national origins
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament stages set in different global locations (Australia, Hong Kong, South Korea, Japan, USA)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Four distinct boss characters with unique designs (Billy Kane - bojutsu master, Axel Hawk - boxer, Laurence Blood - matador, Wolfgang Krauser - German nobleman)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss characters visually distinct from playable roster
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Encountered in tournament finals, representing escalating threat tier

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Referenced through plot: mysterious nobleman sponsor seeks revenge for Geese's death
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Implied through boss character motivations (Billy Kane explicitly seeks revenge for Geese)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Geese legacy drives tournament narrative and boss encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New tournament announced by mysterious nobleman sponsor; shift from original Fatal Fury's South Town setting to global tournament locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss characters actively hunting for the person responsible for defeating Geese; narrative progression through tournament rounds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sequential stage locations change (Australia, Hong Kong, South Korea appearances), representing tournament travel
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player faces eight playable opponents then four boss characters in escalating difficulty; bonus rounds inserted after every fourth match

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Life gauge flashing red at 25% threshold
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Desperation Move becomes available - powerful special move usable only at critical health, indicating 'last resort' combat state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Background plane contains hazards (electrified wires, bull stampede)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player cannot freely change planes; knocking opponent to hazard plane causes extra damage, creating tactical dimension

environmental_storytelling.example: Each stage visually represents a different international location tied to character origin (Australian wrestler's stage, Hong Kong location for Cheng Sinzan, South Korean arena for Kim Kaphwan)
Stage-specific dangers (electrified wires, animal stampedes) communicate environmental character and add tactical stakes to plane-switching mechanic

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character visual design as cultural identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Fighter designs reflect national/martial arts origins: Mai's ninja outfit (Japanese kunoichi), Kim's taekwondo gi, Big Bear's wrestler physique, Cheng Sinzan's tai chi master appearance

Four bosses visually separated from playable roster through unique designs (bojutsu staff user Billy Kane, heavyweight boxer Axel Hawk, matador Laurence Blood, nobleman Wolfgang Krauser) signaling power tier escalation
Bonus round placement after every fourth match and clone character encounter before bosses provide visual/structural markers of campaign progression toward final antagonists

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can taunt opponent from distance (Strong Punch button), displaying confidence
Desperation Move available - access to massive-damage special move indicating 'cornered fighter' desperation state
Normal offensive combos and special moves; no visual tells of vulnerability
Vulnerability window opens for Evasion Attack counterattack, indicating temporary defensive disadvantage
Encountered only after defeating eight playable fighters, signaling extreme threat level through encounter order
Bojutsu fighting style displays revenge motivation against Bogard brothers and Joe through combat design

notes: Fatal Fury 2 (SNES) uses regional censorship differences that affect visual communication: Mai's character design censored in international versions (reduced breast bounce, enlarged thong) and Laurence Blood's name shortened with 'Bloody Spin' voice removed. These censorship changes represent platform/publisher-level world-state shifts rather than in-game systems. The two-plane battle system is core to danger/safety communication—hazard placement on alternate planes creates tactical risk visualization. Character roster internationalization (Korean taekwondo master Kim Kaphwan introduced as first Korean fighting game character) represents narrative world expansion from original game's South Town focus. Tier T2 data relies on gameplay mechanic descriptions and character design analysis rather than numerical metrics.
sources_used: tcrf - Fatal Fury 2 (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
